---
title: 'Define Praise'
date: '2013-03-24T21:21:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Towdah: tō·dä'''
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5570973633994536832'
blogger_filename: '/2013/03/define-praise.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Towdah_ tō·dä_/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Insight'
  - 'Praise/Worship'
---

<br />
<h1 style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; line-height: 19px;">
<a data-mce-href="http://dictionary.reference.com/browse/praise" href="http://dictionary.reference.com/browse/praise" target="_blank">Dictionary.com:</a></h1>
<h1 id="query_h1" style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; line-height: 19px;">
praise</h1>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
–noun</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
1.<div>
the act of expressing approval or admiration; commendation; laudation.</div>
</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
2.<div>
the offering of grateful homage in words or song, as an act of worship:&nbsp;a hymn of praise to&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/browse/god" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/browse/god">god</a>.</div>
</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
3.<div>
the state of being approved or admired:&nbsp;The king lived in praise for many years.</div>
</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
4.<div>
Archaic.&nbsp;a ground for praise, or a merit.</div>
</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong><em>Origin:&nbsp;</em></strong><br />1175–1225;&nbsp;(v.) ME&nbsp;preisen&nbsp;&lt; OF&nbsp;preisier&nbsp;to value, prize &lt; LLpretiāre,&nbsp;deriv. of L&nbsp;pretium&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/browse/price" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/browse/price">price</a>,&nbsp;worth, reward; (n.) ME, deriv. of the v.;&nbsp;see&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/browse/prize" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/browse/prize">prize</a><sup>2</sup></div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
—Synonyms<br />1.&nbsp;acclamation, plaudit, applause, approbation, compliment.&nbsp;2.encomium, eulogy, panegyric.&nbsp;5.&nbsp;laud, applaud, eulogize.&nbsp;See<a data-mce-href="/browse/approve" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/browse/approve">approve.</a>&nbsp;6.&nbsp;glorify, exalt, honor.</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
—Antonyms<br />1.&nbsp;condemnation.&nbsp;5.&nbsp;depreciate.</div>
<h1 style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; line-height: 19px;">
From&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Praise" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Praise" target="_blank">Wikipedia</a>, the free encyclopedia</h1>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
In its common usage,&nbsp;<strong>praise</strong>&nbsp;is the act of making positive statements about a person, object or idea, either in public or privately. Praise is typically, but not exclusively, earned relative to&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/Achievement" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/Achievement" title="Achievement">achievement</a>&nbsp;and accomplishment. Praise is often contrasted with&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/Criticism" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/Criticism" title="Criticism">criticism</a>, where the latter is held to mean exclusively&nbsp;<em>negative</em>&nbsp;statements made about something, although this is not technically correct (see also&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/Blame" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/Blame" title="Blame">Blame</a>).</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
In religion,&nbsp;<strong>praise</strong>&nbsp;is an impassioned&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/Exaltation" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/Exaltation" title="Exaltation">exaltation</a>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/God" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/God" title="God">God</a>&nbsp;(i.e. a&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/Supreme_Being" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/Supreme_Being" title="Supreme Being">Supreme Being</a>, or&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="/wiki/Creation_myth" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wiki/Creation_myth" title="Creation myth">Creation</a>). Praise relates to Gods Greatness. Worshop relates to Gods holyness and Thanks giving to Gods goodness. Praise is the appropriate response to Gods greatness. Praise is Gods address. It doesn't depend on our visible situations or circumstances, feelings. It's base on eternal unchangeable facts. His mercy is everlasting his truth endures to all generations.</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<div>
<a data-mce-href="Praising-hands.svg" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wp-admin/Praising-hands.svg" title="Enlarge"><img alt="" data-mce-src="http://bits.wikimedia.org/skins-1.5/common/images/magnify-clip.png" height="11" src="http://bits.wikimedia.org/skins-1.5/common/images/magnify-clip.png" style="border: 0px; cursor: default;" width="15" /></a></div>
<div>
<h3>
Praise and children</h3>
Common advice is that praise should not be given to children without reason<sup><a data-mce-href="#cite_note-1" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wp-admin/post.php?post=13&amp;action=edit#cite_note-1">[2]</a></sup><sup><a data-mce-href="#cite_note-2" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wp-admin/post.php?post=13&amp;action=edit#cite_note-2">[3]</a></sup>.<br />
<ul>
<li>Reward the attainment of specifed goals, not just participation, of the child.</li>
<li>Praise small changes and successes. Waiting for perfection may take forever.</li>
<li>Be descriptive and specific with praise.</li>
<li>A lot of praise is required to outweigh one criticism. Researchers suggest a ratio of 6:1.</li>
<li>Ensure praise being given to the child is deserved.</li>
<li>Encourage good behaviour with praise, rather than pointing out bad behaviour.</li>
<li>Praise progress is in relation to an individuals past progress, NOT compared with others.</li>
<li>Teachers should NOT praise uninvolved students for not disrupting the class.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<a data-mce-href="http://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/praise" href="http://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/praise"><img alt="Go to homepage." class="alignleft" data-mce-src="http://www.merriam-webster.com/images/hdr_mw_logo_area_160px.jpg" height="119" src="http://www.merriam-webster.com/images/hdr_mw_logo_area_160px.jpg" style="border: 0px; cursor: default; float: left;" width="96" /></a><br />
Etymology: Middle English&nbsp;<em>preisen,</em>&nbsp;from Anglo-French&nbsp;<em>preiser, priser</em>&nbsp;to appraise, esteem — more at&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="prize" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wp-admin/prize">prize</a>&nbsp;Date: 13th century<br />
<em>transitive verb</em><br />
<em><strong>1</strong>&nbsp;<strong>:</strong>&nbsp;to express a favorable judgment of&nbsp;<strong>:</strong>&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="commend" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wp-admin/commend">commend</a><br /><strong>2</strong>&nbsp;<strong>:</strong>&nbsp;to glorify (a god or saint) especially by the attribution of perfections</em><br />
<em></em><em>intransitive verb</em><strong>:</strong>&nbsp;to express praise</div>
